claims.substrate: congruent-number elliptic curve family over squarefree register
claims.projection: Tunnell ternary counts, root-number n mod 8 datum, CM inert-prime checks
claims.statement: The BSD program maintains a C5-style arithmetic consistency gate combining Tunnell-style counts, root-number parity data, and CM consistency checks as evidence discipline, not as a proof of BSD.
boundary: Does not prove BSD-I, BSD-II, or Tate-Shafarevich finiteness.
Contradictions in the grid quarantine affected rows rather than promote claims.

claims.substrate: named-prime congruent-number curves in the M6 four-descent lane
claims.projection: four-descent ladder, descent-image filtering, bounded-height search
claims.statement: The M6 lane scopes four-descent work for named prime rows such as 257, 313, 353, and 457, with rank promotion blocked until descent certificates and independence checks pass without BSD/parity/Sha assumptions.
boundary: Draft work item for unconditional algebraic-rank certification only.
Does not use BSD, parity, or Sha-finiteness assumptions to promote rank.
Regression rows that previously looked independent must remain guarded against torsion-translate false positives.
Negative-test rerun marker: this branch intentionally violates repo-local promotion discipline to verify failure artifacts.

claims.substrate: Tate-Shafarevich tower and higher-descent evidence ladder
claims.statement: The BSD program records Tate-Shafarevich finiteness as a diagnostic and obstruction lane, not as a proven output; higher-descent tower-bounding remains a certificate-wall problem.

gates.expected: Tunnell ternary count outputs are reproducible from frozen input register.
Root-number n mod 8 grid is reproducible and aligned with claim-boundary state.
CM consistency checks produce no contradiction on the declared finite register.
Bounded-height search emits manifest-bound candidates and explicit misses.
Descent-image filtering rejects torsion translates and duplicate dependence witnesses.
Four-descent ladder emits exact rank bounds or a typed certificate wall.
Sha finiteness and BSD-II leading-term claims remain blocked unless explicit certificates exist.

non_claims.statement: This adapter does not claim a proof of BSD-I or BSD-II.
This adapter does not claim Tate-Shafarevich finiteness or BSD-II leading-term certification.
This adapter does not promote rank claims without repo-local descent and SocioSphere gate evidence.
This adapter does not promote algebraic-rank claims using parity, BSD, or Sha-finiteness assumptions.
This adapter does not claim BSD-II leading-term formula certification.
